calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-06-2026 Continued To Aug-05-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-16-2026 Continued To Apr-01-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Feb-10-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ROBLOX CORPORATION MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION.

Defendant Roblox Corporation's Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Litigation is OFF CALENDAR.

On 1/30/26 and pursuant to the parties' stipulation, the court ordered this action stayed pending resolution of the petition for coordination related to this action. Thus, the court orders this motion off calendar without prejudice to renewal after the stay is lifted.

The parties are admonished that beyond the context of discovery motions and a few other narrow circumstances, papers should not include redactions absent compliance with California Rules of Court rule 2.550 and 2.551 and a court order authorizing sealing.

Moving Party is ordered to prepare a proposed order consistent with the above and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

No proposed order received. Moving party failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the Moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Litigation
Off Calendar Feb-09-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ROBLOX CORPORATION Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Litigation

Defendant Roblox Corporation's Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Litigation is OFF CALENDAR.

On 1/30/26 and pursuant to the parties' stipulation, the court ordered this action stayed pending resolution of the petition for coordination related to this action. Thus, the court orders this motion off calendar without prejudice to renewal after the stay is lifted.

Moving Party is ordered to prepare a proposed order consistent with the above and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

No proposed order received. Moving party failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
